marketName: Data Processing, Hosting, and Related Services

features.rationale: (must_be, 6.00): Commodity baseline — every flow alternative in JTBD-2.4 delivers flow quantification. Must_be because a CDU without flow telemetry cannot safely gate GPU load. Primary drivers: Reliability (8) and Confidence (8) — OCP 1.5 L/min/kW threshold (ES-08, imp=9) enforcement; Safety (8) — loss-of-flow detection <2s (ES-11, opp=16). [SRC: JTBD_dph ES-08, ES-11; OCP guidance]

(attractive, 6.56): Directly addresses ES-12 (sub-second thermal duty kW = flow × ΔT from a single body, imp=8, opp=12, prod_related). Eliminates separate temperature probe and synchronization error between two sensors feeding the DCIM PUE calculation. Primary drivers: Cost (8) — removes second sensor BOM and wiring; Pain (7) — single commissioning touchpoint; Delight (6) — "kW out of one can" is genuinely surprising to CDU integrators. [SRC: JTBD_dph ES-12, ES-18]

(one_dimensional, 3.56): CDU secondary-loop pressure drop is dominated by the brazed-plate HX, cold-plate manifolds, and hoses — sensor contribution <50 mbar is marginal at 30 L/min (ES-13, imp=7, opp=8). Linear benefit to pump energy / PUE, but small magnitude. Primary driver: Cost (6) — pump energy savings. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition, JTBD_dph ES-13]

(one_dimensional, 6.78): High-value differentiator vs turbine/impeller incumbents (15% share) in 24×7 GPU fleet. Addresses ES-09 (±2% over 40,000 h, imp=9, opp=13). Bearing wear in turbine sensors silently degrades the flow reading that gates the OCP safety threshold. Primary drivers: Reliability (9) — eliminates mechanical failure modes in the loop that protects GPU thermal envelope; Pain (8) and Cost (8) — removes periodic replacement of in-loop sensors across hundreds of rack CDUs; Confidence (8) — stable calibration reduces false-throttle events (ES-16). [SRC: JTBD_dph ES-09, ES-16]

(attractive, 5.00): Addresses ES-15 (reverse-flow detection within 1s, imp=7, opp=10, prod_related) — detects bypass-valve misoperation, mis-piped commissioning, or pump-failure siphon. Turbine and thermal-dispersion alternatives cannot resolve direction. Primary drivers: Safety (7) — detects anti-flow condition before GPU load energizes; Confidence (6) — unambiguous direction signal in the OCP safety interlock chain. [SRC: JTBD_dph ES-15]

(attractive, 7.56): **Highest-scoring feature.** Directly serves the top of the opportunity stack: empty-pipe pre-IT-power-on (ES-06, opp=15, prod_related), air entrainment after glycol top-up (ES-10, opp=12, prod_related), incipient leak signatures (ES-20, opp=14, prod_related). Traditional mechanical meters provide zero diagnostics. Primary drivers: Safety (9) — the empty-pipe and sub-threshold-leak signals are the gate and early-warning layer that prevent GPU thermal runaway; Confidence (9) — controller knows when a zero-flow reading is real vs diagnostic; Stress (8) — reduces operator anxiety during workload ramps. [SRC: JTBD_dph ES-06, ES-10, ES-20]

(must_be, 4.67): Must_be because CDU secondary loops run water, PG25, or EG25 blends configured at commissioning (ES-03, prod_related). Without speed-of-sound compensation, a glycol-concentration change silently biases kW duty and PUE telemetry. Primary driver: Reliability (7) — reduces fluid-property-induced error from ~1.5% to ~0.2%. [SRC: 01_product_decomposition; JTBD_dph ES-03]

### 09b — Validation Summary

| Check | Result |
|---|---|
| Kano cross-market consistency | N/A — single market |
| safety >= 7 AND confidence <= 3 | PASS — Digital Anomaly Detection: safety=9, confidence=9 (consistent) |
| skill >= 7 AND stress <= 3 | PASS — no violations |
| overall_fit arithmetic | PASS — all recomputed |
| No feature scores 10 on all dimensions | PASS |
| Feature names comply with 1.pdf rules | PASS — spec-visible noun phrases, no brands, no outcome claims |
| Safety-critical coherence | PASS — Transit-Time, No Moving Parts, and Digital Anomaly all score safety >= 7, aligned with market's top 5 needs being safety-driven |
